cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true about folk medicine based on the provided passage. First, I need to look at each option and compare it to the information in the text. Starting with option A: "It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs." The first sentence of the passage says, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." That seems to match exactly. So A looks like a possible correct answer. Option B: "It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals." The passage mentions that folk medicine uses plants and herbs to cure sickness, and that animals use snakeroot after a snake bite. But does it say it can treat "most kinds of diseases"? The text doesn't explicitly state that it can treat most diseases, just that it's used for various purposes. So maybe B is not correct. Option C: "It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases." The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other types of medicine. It doesn't mention effectiveness in serious diseases. So C is probably incorrect. Option D: "It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history."
